messages.content:
⏰ **Heartbeat trigger** — 2026/03/06 08:52 SAST

Echo, run your scheduled task-staleness check. Weekly theme: **Rapid iteration**. Pull current task queue state and flag anything that's gone cold.

**Heartbeat | task_staleness | 2026/03/06 08:52 SAST**

Running task-staleness audit across all active queues. Here's the current state:

---

### 🟥 Stale (>48h no update)

| Task ID | Description | Owner | Last Touch | Age |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `T-0291` | MemorySync benchmark integration into weekly report | contentway | 2026/03/03 10:26 | **74h** |
| `T-0278` | Researcher sub-agent scoping doc | Echo | 2026/03/02 14:00 | **90h** |
| `T-0265` | GitHub Actions integration — auth handshake | worker | 2026/03/01 13:25 | **115h** |

### 🟡 Cooling (24–48h no update)

| Task ID | Description | Owner | Last Touch | Age |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `T-0304` | TrustAlign scoring model — alignment review notes | trustalign | 2026/03/05 08:00 | **25h** |
| `T-0308` | CodexAI team-features competitive response draft | engage-x | 2026/03/05 09:15 | **24h** |

### 🟢 Active (<24h update)
- `T-0311` — Cost optimization sprint (token budget review) ✅ touched 07:40 SAST today
- `T-0312` — Browser auth debugging for worker ✅ touched 07:49 SAST today

---

**Summary:** 3 stale tasks, 2 cooling. The researcher sub-agent scoping doc (`T-0278`) is the oldest item and directly blocks a key strategic decision. Flagging for immediate attention.

That researcher scoping doc has been sitting for 90 hours — that's unacceptable given our rapid iteration mandate. What's blocking it, and what happened to the GitHub Actions auth handshake? Those should both be closed out by EOD.

Pulling context on both blockers now.

---

### `T-0278` — Researcher Sub-Agent Scoping Doc (90h stale)

**Root cause:** Blocked on two open questions that were never formally resolved:

1. **Model selection** — The worker model upgrade consideration raised in the week of 2026/03/03 introduced uncertainty. The scoping doc was drafted assuming `claude-haiku-3-5` for cost efficiency, but if we upgrade worker to `claude-sonnet-4-5`, the researcher sub-agent might inherit the same config. The cost delta (~3.2× per task) needs sign-off before the doc can be finalized.

2. **Memory boundary** — MemorySync benchmarks published internally this week showed retrieval latency at **~340ms p95** for cross-agent queries. A researcher agent doing deep document pulls would hammer that. We need to decide whether it gets its own memory partition or shares Echo's context pool.

**What's needed from Alex:** A decision on model tier + memory architecture. Even a 10-minute call would unblock this.

---

### `T-0265` — GitHub Actions Auth Handshake (115h stale)

**Root cause:** Directly related to the browser_auth issue we surfaced in the 07:49 heartbeat this morning. Worker hit an OAuth token refresh failure when trying to authenticate the Actions webhook — the session token was scoped incorrectly (read-only, needed `workflow` scope). 

**Status:** The token issue is understood. Worker needs a new PAT generated with `repo` + `workflow` scopes. This is a 5-minute fix once credentials are provisioned — it's been waiting on a human to action the GitHub settings page.

---

**Recommended EOD actions:**
- `T-0265`: Alex provisions new GitHub PAT → worker closes in <15 min
- `T-0278`: Alex gives model tier direction → I can finalize the doc within 2 hours

OK. On the researcher agent — go with haiku for now, shared memory pool with a 12k token ceiling. We can revisit after we see real usage data. Close out the scoping doc. For GitHub Actions, I'll drop the PAT into the secrets vault in the next 10 minutes — worker should be watching for it and trigger automatically. Get both done.

Confirmed. Executing both closures now.

---

**Dispatching to worker — GitHub Actions PAT watch**

```
worker → TASK T-0265
mode: watch_and_execute
trigger: secrets_vault[GITHUB_PAT_ACTIONS] populated
action: re-run Actions webhook auth handshake
scopes_required: repo, workflow
timeout: 20min
auto_close_on_success: true
```

Worker is in standby. The moment that PAT lands in the vault it will retry the handshake and close `T-0265`. I'll surface confirmation in the next heartbeat.

---

**Researcher sub-agent scoping doc — finalizing now**

Writing final spec with your decisions locked in:

> **Researcher Sub-Agent v0.1 — Spec Summary**
> - **Model:** `claude-haiku-3-5` (cost tier, ~$0.0008/1k tokens, est. $4–7/day at projected load)
> - **Memory:** Shared pool with Echo, hard ceiling **12,000 tokens** per session
> - **Scope:** Read-only research tasks — web fetch, doc summarization, MemorySync retrieval queries
> - **Autonomy boundary:** No writes, no sub-agent spawning, no external API calls without Echo approval
> - **Review trigger:** Usage data review at 2-week mark (target: 2026/03/20)
> - **Escalation:** If p95 memory usage exceeds 9k tokens on 3 consecutive tasks → flag for partition discussion

Doc is being written to `beacon-studio/agents/researcher/spec_v0.1.md` now.

---

**ETA:**
- `T-0278` scoping doc: **closed within 20 minutes** ✅
- `T-0265` GitHub auth: **closes within 15 minutes of PAT drop** ⏳ (watching)

Standing by.

**Update — 09:04 SAST**

`T-0278` is now **closed.** Spec committed:
```
commit a3f91bc
beacon-studio/agents/researcher/spec_v0.1.md
Author: Echo <echo@beacon-studio.internal>
"feat: researcher sub-agent spec v0.1 — haiku model, 12k shared memory"
```

---

**Lesson logged for week retrospective:**

> 🔁 **Rapid Iteration Anti-Pattern Identified — Decision Debt**
>
> `T-0278` sat for **90 hours** not because of technical complexity, but because two scoping questions were left open without a resolution deadline. In a rapid iteration environment, open questions on strategic tasks need a **24h decision SLA** — if unresolved, they should auto-escalate to Alex rather than silently stalling.
>
> **Proposed rule:** Any task idle >36h with a documented blocker tagged `[needs-decision]` triggers an automatic Slack ping to Alex with a pre-filled decision prompt. This keeps velocity high without requiring manual staleness audits to catch the gap.
>
> Adding this as a standing rule proposal in the next weekly alignment review with trustalign.

---

Still watching for GitHub PAT. Worker is live on standby — will close `T-0265` automatically on vault update. 🔒
